region_of_origin: United States (commercial origin ~1990s); widely available in Singapore supermarkets and cafés from ~2010
food_subgroup: Commercial unsweetened nut-based plant milk alternative (almond-based); approximately 2% almond content by weight; typically fortified with calcium, vitamin D, and vitamin E

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for commercial unsweetened almond milk. Closest parent concept: nut-based plant beverage / plant milk alternative. URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Almond milk, commercial unsweetened — nut-based plant beverage; closest-parent match within FoodEx2 plant-based beverage hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0NTB is the closest defensible FoodEx2 code for nut-based plant beverages. No exact code for commercial unsweetened almond milk exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for commercial almond milk beverage. Not assigned to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: Fortified with calcium, vitamin D, vitamin E
main_ingredients: Stabilisers (e.g. gellan gum, locust bean gum)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 250 ml (approximately one cup), equivalent to the portion used when substituting dairy milk in coffee, cereal, or smoothies.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, almond milk, unsweetened, shelf stable — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; cross-referenced with Singapore brand label analysis (Sanitarium So Good Almond Unsweetened, Alpro Almond No Sugars, Silk Unsweetened Almond)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (primary); Singapore brand label analysis (secondary cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FDC provides the unfortified almond milk baseline. Singapore commercial brands (Sanitarium So Good, Alpro, Silk) are fortified; calcium (~120 mg/100 ml), vitamin D (~1 mcg/100 ml), and vitamin E (~3.5 mg/100 ml) values reflect the fortified commercial product sold in Singapore. Sodium (~62 mg/100 ml) reflects the average of Singapore brand labels. All values are per 100 ml (liquid food; density ~1.0 g/ml, so per 100 ml ≈ per 100 g).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Atkinson et al. 2008; University of Sydney GI Database (almond milk, unsweetened, analogous low-carbohydrate plant beverage)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~25 is an analogous estimate for unsweetened almond milk based on its extremely low carbohydrate content (~0.5 g/100 ml; ~1.25 g per 250 ml serving). The University of Sydney GI Database does not list a direct entry for commercial unsweetened almond milk; GI ~25 is consistent with published estimates for very-low-carbohydrate plant beverages and is widely cited in peer-reviewed literature (e.g., Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). GL per 250 ml serving = (25 × 1.25) / 100 = 0.31, rounded to 0.3 — classified as negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~155 mg per 250 ml serving) and very low caloric density (38 kcal per 250 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened almond milk is suitable for hypertensive patients as a dairy milk substitute; its sodium content (~155 mg per 250 ml) is low-to-moderate and its very low caloric density supports weight management relevant to hypertension-obesity comorbidity, though potassium content (~150 mg per 250 ml) is modest and does not provide meaningful antihypertensive potassium benefit compared to dairy milk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic load (GL ~0.3 per 250 ml serving) and high vitamin E content (~8.75 mg per 250 ml)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened almond milk is the lowest-GL plant milk available (GL ~0.3 per 250 ml versus oat milk GL ~8 per 250 ml) and is strongly preferred over sweetened or higher-carbohydrate plant milks for patients with Type 2 Diabetes; its high vitamin E content (~8.75 mg per 250 ml, ~58% of RDI) provides antioxidant benefit relevant to oxidative stress associated with T2DM, though its very low protein content (1.25 g per 250 ml) means it cannot substitute dairy milk for protein requirements.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.25 g per 250 ml), zero cholesterol, and high vitamin E antioxidant content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unsweetened almond milk is a heart-favourable dairy milk substitute for cardiovascular disease patients due to its negligible saturated fat (0.25 g per 250 ml versus ~4 g in full-fat dairy), zero dietary cholesterol, and high vitamin E content (~8.75 mg per 250 ml) which supports LDL oxidation protection; however, its very low protein and potassium content mean it should not be the sole nutritional substitute for dairy in a cardiac diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High vitamin E antioxidant content and fortified calcium relevant to steroid-associated osteoporosis prevention
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Unsweetened almond milk provides ~8.75 mg vitamin E per 250 ml serving (a major lipid-soluble antioxidant that reduces lipid peroxidation in joint tissue relevant to RA oxidative stress) and ~300 mg fortified calcium per 250 ml (relevant for RA patients on corticosteroids at risk of osteoporosis); however, its very low protein content (1.25 g per 250 ml) means it cannot replace dairy milk for RA protein requirements.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Almond milk (commercial, unsweetened) is a plant-based dairy milk alternative made by blending blanched almonds with water and straining out the solids, yielding a thin, mildly nutty-flavoured white liquid with approximately 2% almond content by weight. It is sold in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and specialty cafés under brands including Sanitarium So Good, Alpro Almond No Sugars, and Silk Unsweetened Almond. Per 100 ml: 15 kcal (very low energy density), 0.5 g protein (approximately 6× less than dairy milk), 1.1 g fat (predominantly unsaturated), 0.5 g carbohydrate, 62 mg sodium. Per 250 ml serving: ~38 kcal, ~1.25 g protein, ~2.75 g fat, ~1.25 g carbohydrate, ~155 mg sodium. Commercial versions are fortified with calcium (~120 mg/100 ml; ~300 mg per 250 ml serving, comparable to dairy milk), vitamin D (~1 mcg/100 ml), and vitamin E (~3.5 mg/100 ml; ~8.75 mg per 250 ml serving, approximately 58% of adult RDI). Glycemic index ~25 (low); glycemic load per 250 ml serving ~0.3 (negligible) — the lowest GL of all common plant milks, making it strongly preferred over oat milk (GL ~8) or rice milk (GL ~22) for glycaemic management. Suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. Key clinical note: almond milk is not a protein-equivalent substitute for dairy milk and should not be used as the sole dairy replacement in populations with high protein requirements (children, elderly, athletes, RA patients).
common_user_queries: almond milk protein vs dairy milk
almond milk vs oat milk diabetes
best plant milk for diabetes Singapore
meal_context: Dairy milk substitute in coffee and tea (café plant milk Singapore)

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central (approved primary source) and cross-referenced against Singapore commercial brand labels (Sanitarium So Good, Alpro, Silk). Fortified nutrient values (calcium, vitamin D, vitamin E) reflect the commercial Singapore product. GI is an analogous estimate consistent with peer-reviewed literature and the University of Sydney GI Database methodology. All values are internally consistent and plausible for a commercial unsweetened almond milk product.
notes: Sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and Singapore brand labels for unsweetened formulations — naturally zero by product formulation (no added sugar; almonds contain negligible free sugars at ~2% inclusion rate). Vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero for commercial almond milk — naturally zero as almonds are not a vitamin C source and commercial unsweetened almond milk is not fortified with vitamin C. Thiamin_b1 is confirmed zero per USDA FDC for unsweetened almond milk — naturally zero at the almond inclusion levels used in commercial products. Cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per brand label analysis. Water_g is not reported by primary sources for this liquid food and is set to -1. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values (0.7 g and 0.3 g per 100 ml respectively) are estimated from the almond fat profile at ~2% almond inclusion, consistent with USDA almond fatty acid data. FoodEx2 code A0NTB is a closest-parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for commercial unsweetened almond milk. The 250 ml serving is the standard Singapore retail and café portion. Protein content (0.5 g/100 ml; 1.25 g/250 ml) is critically low compared to dairy milk (3.3 g/100 ml; 8.3 g/250 ml) — this is the primary nutritional limitation of almond milk as a dairy substitute.
